key_insight: Danus scales research-level mathematical reasoning by coordinating multiple agents around a shared fact graph that stores verified claims, proofs, and dependencies.
engineering_relevance: The design offers a practical orchestration pattern for long-horizon theorem-proving systems, enabling parallel search while keeping memory, consistency, and human oversight manageable.
method: Main agent plans, summarizes progress, and redirects search across promising proof directions.
Worker agents run parallel proof search on subproblems.
Stateless verifier checks claims before they enter the shared fact graph as trusted memory.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports six research-level case studies across algebraic geometry, singularity theory, and combinatorics, used to illustrate long proof construction.
watch_item: Performance likely depends on verifier reliability and fact-graph integrity, so weak claim checking could propagate errors through long proof chains.
paper_title: Danus: Orchestrating Mathematical Reasoning Agents with Fact-Graph Memory
